title: Implement c5c3-operator ControlPlane for Keystone
description:
# GitHub Issue #389: feat(c5c3-operator): initial implementation — ControlPlane orchestration for Keystone with K-ORC and the admin Application Credential

**Description:**

This issue turns the inert `operators/c5c3/` stub into a working orchestration operator that reconciles a single `ControlPlane` CR into a running Keystone (the operator/CRD/Helm chart that already exists under `operators/keystone/`) and brings up K-ORC (`openstack-resource-controller`) self-credentialed via its own admin Application Credential. This is the first vertical slice of the c5c3-operator. K-ORC and the `KORCSpec`/`AdminCredentialSpec` shape are baked in from the start, replacing the older #364–#368 drafts that pre-dated the credential-model decision and never mentioned K-ORC.

The slice is intentionally Keystone-only: the same reconciler shape is meant to extend to Nova/Neutron/Glance/Cinder/Placement later, but those operators do not yet exist in this repo (`operators/` contains `c5c3` and `keystone` only — see `go.work`/`Makefile:15` `OPERATORS ?= keystone c5c3`), so they are out of scope here.

Concrete boundaries between what exists and what this issue adds:

1. **The `operators/c5c3/` module exists as a manager-only stub.** `operators/c5c3/main.go:32-41` calls `bootstrap.Run` with `LeaderElectionID: "c5c3.openstack.c5c3.io"` and a `SetupFunc` whose body is the `// +kubebuilder:scaffold:builder` marker — no controllers are registered, no schemes added beyond `clientgoscheme`, and there are no `api/`, `internal/`, `config/`, `helm/`, or `Dockerfile` directories under `operators/c5c3/`. The module's `go.mod` (`operators/c5c3/go.mod`) already depends on `github.com/c5c3/forge/internal/common` and `sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime v0.24.1`, so the shared bootstrap, scheme, and conditions packages are already importable.

2. **CI is wired for c5c3 but has nothing to test.** `.github/workflows/ci.yaml:286` and `:321` already list `c5c3` in the `target: [common, keystone, c5c3]` matrices for both `test` and `test-integration`; `Makefile:15` defaults `OPERATORS ?= keystone c5c3` so `make build`, `make test`, `make generate`, `make manifests`, and `make sync-crds` already iterate over c5c3 with the `if [ -d "operators/$$op/api" ]` guard (e.g. `Makefile:220-225`). However, `.github/workflows/ci.yaml:132` still sets `ALL_OPERATORS: keystone` (the matrix consumed by `hack/ci-resolve-changes.sh:42`), so the `build-e2e-images`/`e2e-operator` matrix only covers keystone today. Adding `c5c3` to that env, adding a `c5c3:` paths-filter block (`.github/workflows/ci.yaml:76-78` is the `keystone:` template), and wiring an `operators/c5c3/Dockerfile` path into `.github/workflows/build-images.yaml:46-56` (`lint-dockerfiles` matrix) is part of this scope.

3. **K-ORC is not present anywhere.** A literal grep for `openstack-resource-controller`, `k-orc`, `K-ORC`, `orc-system`, or `k-orc-clouds-yaml` returns zero matches outside `.planwerk/` snapshots. There is no K-ORC HelmRelease, no `applicationcredentials.openstack.k-orc.cloud` CRD, no `orc-system` Namespace in `deploy/flux-system/namespaces.yaml`, and no ExternalSecret materialising `k-orc-clouds-yaml`. The K-ORC deployment, the namespace, the ESO wiring, and the operator-side `ApplicationCredential`/`Service`/`Endpoint` CR projection are all new.

4. **The Keystone CRD that the c5c3 reconciler will own already exists.** The `Keystone` type is defined at `operators/keystone/api/v1alpha1/keystone_types.go:33-39` (group `keystone.openstack.c5c3.io`, version `v1alpha1`, see `operators/keystone/api/v1alpha1/groupversion_info.go:22`). `KeystoneSpec` (`keystone_types.go:51-225`) already accepts the fields the c5c3 reconciler must project from `ControlPlaneSpec.services.keystone`: `Replicas`, `Image`, `Database`/`Cache` with `clusterRef`-or-brownfield modes (`internal/common/types/types.go:20-77`), `Fernet.RotationSchedule`/`MaxActiveKeys`, `PolicyOverrides` (`keystone_types.go:110-116`), `Bootstrap.Region`, and the rest. The image tag must be derived from `openStackRelease` (the operator's `appVersion`/release matrix in `releases/` and the keystone image at `images/keystone/Dockerfile`); the existing `commonv1.ImageSpec{Repository, Tag}` shape carries that directly.

5. **MariaDB and Memcached are the only infrastructure backends Keystone needs and both have working CRs.** `deploy/flux-system/infrastructure/mariadb.yaml:24-66` defines a `MariaDB` (`k8s.mariadb.com/v1alpha1`) Galera+MaxScale cluster with `serverCertIssuerRef`/`clientCertIssuerRef` pointing at `openstack-db-ca-issuer`; `deploy/flux-system/infrastructure/memcached.yaml:11-17` defines a `Memcached` (`memcached.c5c3.io/v1beta1`, 3 replicas). The `mariadb-operator` and `memcached-operator` HelmReleases that own those CRDs are listed in `deploy/flux-system/kustomization.yaml:43-50` and already declared as dependencies of `deploy/flux-system/releases/keystone-operator.yaml:22-28`. The c5c3 reconciler's Phase 1 ("`reconcileInfrastructure`") needs to project `spec.infrastructure.{database,cache}` into MariaDB/Memcached CRs and own them — the names, namespace (`openstack`), and shape are already proven by the existing static manifests above and should be the reference baseline.

6. **The ESO → OpenBao chain is fully bootstrapped.** `deploy/eso/clustersecretstore.yaml` defines the `openbao-cluster-store` `ClusterSecretStore` that the rest of the repo references (e.g. `deploy/eso/externalsecrets/keystone-admin.yaml:18-21`, `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_secrets.go:25-30`). `deploy/openbao/policies/eso-management.hcl:11-36` grants `read` on `kv-v2/data/bootstrap/*`, `kv-v2/data/infrastructure/*`, and `kv-v2/data/openstack/keystone/*`; `deploy/openbao/policies/push-keystone-keys.hcl:73-86` grants `create/update/patch` on `kv-v2/data/openstack/keystone/+/{fernet,credential}-keys`; `deploy/openbao/policies/push-app-credentials.hcl:9-11` already grants `create/update/read` on `kv-v2/data/openstack/*/app-credential` — the K-ORC admin App Cred path will plug into that existing policy file without a new HCL grant if the path is chosen consistently (e.g. `kv-v2/data/openstack/keystone/admin/app-credential`). The OpenBao bootstrap scripts at `deploy/openbao/bootstrap/{init-unseal,setup-policies,setup-auth,write-bootstrap-secrets}.sh` already seed `kv-v2/bootstrap/keystone-admin` (`deploy/openbao/bootstrap/write-bootstrap-secrets.sh:115-120`), which is the root of trust this issue depends on.

7. **The pattern for "operator-owned Secret → ESO `PushSecret` → OpenBao → `ExternalSecret` → tenant Secret" is already implemented and is the template the K-ORC slice mirrors.** The split-compute-write apply pattern lives in `reconcile_fernet.go:43-217` (Fernet) and `reconcile_credential.go` (credential keys); the admin-rotation Model B path in `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_passwordrotation.go:568-610` builds a `PushSecret` with `SecretStoreRefs[]{Kind: "ClusterSecretStore", Name: "openbao-cluster-store"}` and `RemoteKey: "bootstrap/keystone-admin"` via the shared `secrets.EnsurePushSecret` helper (`internal/common/secrets/secrets.go:128`). The K-ORC admin-App-Cred sub-reconciler must mirror this shape: mint the credential via a K-ORC `ApplicationCredential` CR (managed mode), commit it to an operator-owned Secret, push it to OpenBao at `openstack/keystone/admin/app-credential`, then let an ExternalSecret materialise `k-orc-clouds-yaml` into `orc-system`. The `secrets.WaitForExternalSecret` + `secrets.IsClusterSecretStoreReady` helpers (`internal/common/secrets/secrets.go:43-58`) are reusable as the readiness gate.

8. **There is no `ControlPlane`, `KORCSpec`, `AdminCredentialSpec`, `CredentialRotation`, or `SecretAggregate` type defined anywhere.** Grep on `ControlPlaneSpec`/`KORCSpec`/`AdminCredentialSpec`/`CredentialRotation` returns no matches outside `.planwerk/` snapshots. All Go types, deepcopy methods (`zz_generated.deepcopy.go`), defaulting/validation webhooks, generated CRD YAMLs under `operators/c5c3/config/crd/bases/`, and Helm `crds/` copies under `operators/c5c3/helm/c5c3-operator/crds/` are created fresh by this issue. The `Makefile:238-246` `manifests` target and `Makefile:259-274` `sync-crds` target already iterate over `$(OPERATORS)` and `if [ -d "operators/$$op/api" ]`, so once `operators/c5c3/api/v1alpha1/` exists the CRDs regenerate without further Makefile changes.

9. **The reference reconciler pattern this issue must follow lives in `operators/keystone/internal/controller/keystone_controller.go`.** That controller embeds `client.Client`, `Scheme`, `Recorder` (see `keystone_controller.go:122-135` shape), exposes `SetupWithManager` (`keystone_controller.go:687-762`) with `Owns(...)` on every child it manages plus `Watches(...)+EnqueueRequestsFromMapFunc(...)` for resources it does not own (Secrets, MariaDB, ClusterSecretStore, PushSecrets), and runs sub-reconcilers in dependency order with an early return on non-zero `ctrl.Result` (`keystone_controller.go:185-411`). Each sub-reconciler returns `(ctrl.Result, error)`, sets its own Status Condition via the shared `internal/common/conditions.SetCondition` helper (`internal/common/conditions/conditions.go:15-19`), and is wrapped in `instrumentSubReconciler` for per-reconciler Prometheus metrics (`operators/keystone/internal/controller/instrumentation.go:46-78`). The aggregate `Ready` condition is computed from `subConditionTypes` (`keystone_controller.go:102-119`). The c5c3 `ControlPlaneReconciler` should follow this exact shape: own MariaDB, Memcached, Keystone, and the K-ORC `ApplicationCredential`/`Service`/`Endpoint` CRs; declare its own `subConditionTypes = {InfrastructureReady, KeystoneReady, KORCReady, AdminCredentialReady, CatalogReady}` (the `ServiceUsersReady` condition listed in the issue body is deferred); wrap each phase in `instrumentSubReconciler` with a `c5c3_*` metric prefix mirroring the keystone metrics package (`operators/keystone/internal/metrics`).

10. **The shared bootstrap, conditions, secrets, and job packages are operator-agnostic and ready to consume.** `internal/common/bootstrap/bootstrap.go` exposes `ManagerConfig{Scheme, LeaderElectionID, Namespace, SetupFunc}` and `Run(...)` (`bootstrap.go:90+`); `internal/common/conditions/conditions.go:15-43` provides `SetCondition`/`GetCondition`/`IsReady`/`AllTrue`; `internal/common/secrets/secrets.go:43-149` provides `WaitForExternalSecret`, `IsClusterSecretStoreReady`, `GetSecretValue`, `EnsurePushSecret`, and `IsMissingSecretOrKey`; `internal/common/types` provides `ImageSpec`, `DatabaseSpec`, `CacheSpec`, `SecretRefSpec`, `PolicySpec` that the c5c3 CRD reuses verbatim (the keystone CRD reuses them via `commonv1` — see `keystone_types.go:72-83`). Re-use of these types keeps the projection from `ControlPlaneSpec.{infrastructure,services.keystone}` → `KeystoneSpec` a near-trivial struct copy rather than a translation layer.

11. **A `c5c3-operator` HelmRelease is not in the GitOps stack.** `deploy/flux-system/releases/` contains 8 entries today (`cert-manager.yaml`, `external-secrets.yaml`, `keystone-operator.yaml`, `mariadb-operator-crds.yaml`, `mariadb-operator.yaml`, `memcached-operator.yaml`, `openbao.yaml`, `prometheus-operator-crds.yaml`); `deploy/flux-system/kustomization.yaml:43-50` enumerates them. There is no `c5c3-operator.yaml` and no `c5c3-system` Namespace in `deploy/flux-system/namespaces.yaml:18-58` (the latter currently declares `cert-manager`, `mariadb-system`, `external-secrets`, `monitoring`, `memcached-system`, `openstack`, `openbao-system`, `keystone-system`). Adding the namespace, the HelmRelease (with `dependsOn: [keystone-operator, external-secrets, mariadb-operator, memcached-operator]`), and the resource entry to the kustomization is in scope; the existing `c5c3-charts` OCI HelmRepository (`deploy/flux-system/sources/c5c3-charts.yaml`, URL `oci://ghcr.io/c5c3/charts`) is already in place and is the registry the chart publishes to via `.github/workflows/ci.yaml:1263` `helm push`.

12. **The Helm-chart template inventory is reusable.** `operators/keystone/helm/keystone-operator/{Chart.yaml, values.yaml, values.schema.json, templates/{deployment,service,serviceaccount,clusterrole,clusterrolebinding,role,rolebinding,webhook-configuration,certificate,networkpolicy,servicemonitor}.yaml, templates/tests/*}` is the reference layout. The c5c3 chart can be cloned and stripped down to the matching surface: a `Deployment` with `--leader-elect`/`--metrics-bind-address`/`--health-probe-bind-address` (same `bootstrap.Run` flags as keystone), `Service` for metrics+webhook, `ServiceAccount`, `ClusterRole`/`ClusterRoleBinding` derived from the new `+kubebuilder:rbac` markers on the `ControlPlaneReconciler`, `MutatingWebhookConfiguration`+`ValidatingWebhookConfiguration` with `cert-manager.io/inject-ca-from`, and a `certificate.yaml` for the webhook TLS. The `values.schema.json` shape (JSON Schema draft-07, additionalProperties=false) and the Helm test inventory under `templates/tests/*_test.yaml` are CRD/values-validation guards proven by CC-0069 and the existing chart tests (per the Helm Chart Testing pattern).

13. **The `CredentialRotation` CRD is new and narrowly scoped.** The issue body restricts `CredentialRotation` to `target: adminApplicationCredential` only — not Fernet, not credential-keys, not workload service users (those rotate via the existing Keystone-operator CronJobs in `reconcile_fernet.go`/`reconcile_credential.go`/`reconcile_passwordrotation.go`). The acceptance criteria mark the **scheduled** re-mint reconciler as a fast-follow candidate; the **bootstrap + re-mint on password change** path is mandatory for this slice. The implementation surface is a single sub-reconciler that watches the admin password Secret (or its hash on the `ApplicationCredential` CR's pod-template-style annotation, mirroring `adminPasswordHashAnnotation` at `reconcile_bootstrap.go:38`) and re-mints the App Cred via K-ORC's `ApplicationCredential` CR when the password rotates.

14. **The "admin password / App Cred never reaches a workload pod" invariant must be enforced in code.** Today `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_bootstrap.go:182-193` injects `BOOTSTRAP_PASSWORD` only into the one-shot bootstrap Job; the running Keystone Deployment never mounts it (see `reconcile_deployment.go`). The c5c3 operator must preserve this: the admin password Secret is read only inside the c5c3 operator (controller pod) and the K-ORC controller pod (via `k-orc-clouds-yaml` materialised in `orc-system`); it must not be mounted in the Keystone Deployment, the K-ORC client-facing workloads, or any service-user pod. A test should walk all Deployments/StatefulSets/DaemonSets owned (transitively) by the `ControlPlane` and assert their PodSpec has no env/volume reference to `keystone-admin` or the App Cred Secret outside the explicitly allowed pods.

15. **Tests live in three layers and the conventions are fixed.** Unit tests sit next to source files in `*_test.go` (e.g. `keystone_webhook_test.go`, `reconcile_database_test.go`), `envtest` integration tests use `//go:build integration` (e.g. `operators/keystone/internal/controller/integration_test.go`) and are matrix-driven by `make test-integration OPERATOR=...` (`Makefile:309-345`), Chainsaw E2E suites live under `tests/e2e/<operator>/<feature>/chainsaw-test.yaml` and follow a strict "one feature = one directory" convention (currently 44 directories under `tests/e2e/keystone/`, see `ls tests/e2e/keystone/ | wc -l`). The c5c3 E2E suite belongs under `tests/e2e/c5c3/full-controlplane-keystone/`, with the chainsaw runner pulled in via the existing matrix in `.github/workflows/ci.yaml:699-777` once `c5c3` is added to `ALL_OPERATORS`.

16. **Status condition naming follows the project's typed-constant convention.** The keystone reconciler declares `conditionType*Ready` constants for every typed condition (e.g. `reconcile_passwordrotation.go:53-55` `conditionTypePasswordRotationReady`, `reconcile_healthcheck.go` for `conditionTypeKeystoneAPIReady`, etc.) and aggregates them in `subConditionTypes` (`keystone_controller.go:102-119`). The c5c3 operator must adopt the same shape: declare `conditionTypeInfrastructureReady`, `conditionTypeKeystoneReady`, `conditionTypeKORCReady`, `conditionTypeAdminCredentialReady`, `conditionTypeCatalogReady` as typed constants in one file (probably `internal/controller/controlplane_controller.go`), reference them from both production code and tests via the constants (never inline string literals — see the project's "Extract repeated condition strings into typed constants" pattern).

17. **Two helpers called out in the issue body — `intervalToCron` and `projectPolicyOverrides` — have no current equivalent.** Neither name appears in the codebase. `intervalToCron` converts `"168h"` → `"0 0 * * 0"` and is needed to translate `ControlPlaneSpec.…rotationInterval` (Go `time.Duration`) into the cron expressions the Keystone CR's `Fernet.RotationSchedule`/`CredentialKeys.RotationSchedule` accept (`keystone_types.go:336`, `:348`). `projectPolicyOverrides` merges `ControlPlaneSpec.global.policy` with `services.keystone.policyOverrides` into the `commonv1.PolicySpec{Rules,ConfigMapRef}` shape (`internal/common/types/types.go:110-121`) that Keystone's `PolicyOverrides` field consumes. Both are pure functions, table-driven unit-testable, and should live next to the reconciler (e.g. `operators/c5c3/internal/controller/helpers.go`).

18. **The `SecretAggregate` CRD types-only stub is allowed but the reconciler is deferred.** The issue is explicit that `SecretAggregate` may be scaffolded as Go types + deepcopy + CRD YAML, but no reconciler is registered. This means the Helm chart RBAC must not (yet) grant write verbs on `secretaggregates.c5c3.io` — only the type lives, awaiting CC-0023 follow-up. Concretely: define the `SecretAggregate` Go type and CRD YAML; do not register a controller in `main.go`; do not include `+kubebuilder:rbac` markers for `secretaggregates`.

**Motivation:**

`operators/c5c3/` is the orchestration seam the rest of the architecture assumes exists but currently does not. Every downstream piece — Nova/Neutron/Glance/Cinder reconcilers, the multi-service `ControlPlane` lifecycle, the credential-rotation roadmap (planwerk P0/P1 → P2/P3), the documented "ControlPlane → infra → services → catalog" sequence in `docs/reference/keystone/keystone-upgrade-flow.md:723` and `docs/index.md:16` — depends on this slice being real before any second service is added. Shipping it Keystone-first matches the project's stated Keystone-first strategy (`README.md:12-15`) and lets the same operator skeleton extend to Nova later without re-architecting the credential model.

Folding K-ORC into the **initial** vertical slice (rather than after the fact) is the critical change versus the older #364–#368 drafts: those drafts predate the settled K-ORC + admin-Application-Credential design and `gh issue view 366 | grep -i korc` correctly returns nothing. Reconciling Keystone without K-ORC produces a control plane that has identity but no managed catalog and no path to project/role/domain bootstrap that survives recreation — every later service operator would have to invent its own credential bootstrap, contradicting the "K-ORC is the single OpenStack client" architecture decision. By minting K-ORC's admin App Cred from the admin password (the existing root of trust at `kv-v2/bootstrap/keystone-admin` — see `deploy/openbao/bootstrap/write-bootstrap-secrets.sh:115`) and routing it through OpenBao → ESO → `orc-system`, this slice establishes the credential-lifecycle backbone (planwerk P0+P1) on which every subsequent service operator depends.

The blast-radius cost of waiting is high: every week without `ControlPlane` reconciliation means deployments use the existing hand-applied `deploy/flux-system/infrastructure/{mariadb,memcached}.yaml` static CRs plus a Keystone CR hand-crafted in `openstack`. That pattern doesn't compose to multi-service deployments, doesn't survive cluster recreation cleanly, and doesn't carry the K-ORC + admin-App-Cred invariants that the later service operators will assume. Landing this slice closes the gap with one well-bounded change.

**Affected Areas:**

- `operators/c5c3/api/v1alpha1/` (new) — `ControlPlane`/`ControlPlaneList`/`ControlPlaneSpec`/`ControlPlaneStatus`, `KORCSpec`/`AdminCredentialSpec`, `CredentialRotation`/`CredentialRotationList`, optional types-only `SecretAggregate`, plus `groupversion_info.go` (group `c5c3.io`, version `v1alpha1`), `controlplane_webhook.go` defaulter+validator, and `zz_generated.deepcopy.go` (controller-gen).
- `operators/c5c3/internal/controller/controlplane_controller.go` (new) — `ControlPlaneReconciler` (embeds `client.Client`, `Scheme`, `Recorder`) with phased sub-reconcilers `reconcileInfrastructure`, `reconcileKeystone`, `reconcileKORC` (which itself splits into mint-admin-App-Cred → ESO push → import-bootstrap-resources → register-catalog steps), plus `SetupWithManager` with `Owns(&mariadbv1alpha1.MariaDB{})`, `Owns(&memcachedv1beta1.Memcached{})`, `Owns(&keystonev1alpha1.Keystone{})`, and `Owns(&orcv1alpha1.ApplicationCredential{})`/`Service{}`/`Endpoint{}` (K-ORC CRD types), plus `Watches(&corev1.Secret{}, …)` for the admin password Secret with a MapFunc/field-indexer pair mirroring `secretToKeystoneMapper` (`operators/keystone/internal/controller/keystone_controller.go:779-826`).
- `operators/c5c3/internal/controller/reconcile_credentialrotation.go` (new) — `CredentialRotationReconciler` handling the `target: adminApplicationCredential` shape: bootstrap + re-mint on password change. The scheduled `intervalDays`/`preRotationDays`/`gracePeriodDays` loop is allowed to land as a fast-follow if it bloats the PR (the issue body explicitly authorises this).
- `operators/c5c3/internal/controller/helpers.go` (new) — `intervalToCron(time.Duration) (string, error)` and `projectPolicyOverrides(global *commonv1.PolicySpec, perService *commonv1.PolicySpec) *commonv1.PolicySpec` with table-driven unit tests in `helpers_test.go`.
- `operators/c5c3/main.go` — register the new schemes (`c5c3v1alpha1`, `keystonev1alpha1`, `mariadbv1alpha1`, `memcachedv1beta1`, `esov1`/`esov1alpha1`, `orcv1alpha1`), wire `ControlPlaneReconciler` and `CredentialRotationReconciler`, register the defaulting+validation webhooks behind the `webhooks` parameter (mirrors `operators/keystone/main.go:52-69`), and remove the `// +kubebuilder:scaffold:builder` placeholder body.
- `operators/c5c3/Dockerfile` (new) — multi-stage `golang:1.26` builder → `gcr.io/distroless/static:nonroot` runtime, build context the workspace root, mirroring `operators/keystone/Dockerfile:1-30`. Add to the `lint-dockerfiles` matrix at `.github/workflows/build-images.yaml:46-56`.
- `operators/c5c3/config/{crd,webhook}/` (new) — generated CRD YAMLs (`controlplanes.c5c3.io.yaml`, `credentialrotations.c5c3.io.yaml`, optionally `secretaggregates.c5c3.io.yaml`) emitted by `make manifests` (`Makefile:238-246`) and synced into `operators/c5c3/helm/c5c3-operator/crds/` via `make sync-crds` (`Makefile:259-274`); both work today via the `if [ -d "operators/$$op/api" ]` guard.
- `operators/c5c3/helm/c5c3-operator/` (new) — `Chart.yaml`, `values.yaml`, `values.schema.json`, `templates/{deployment,service,serviceaccount,clusterrole,clusterrolebinding,role,rolebinding,webhook-configuration,certificate}.yaml`, `templates/_helpers.tpl`, `templates/tests/*_test.yaml`, `crds/`, mirroring the keystone-operator chart layout under `operators/keystone/helm/keystone-operator/`.
- `internal/common/secrets/` — no new exported helpers expected, but verify `EnsurePushSecret` (`secrets.go:128`), `WaitForExternalSecret` (`secrets.go:43`), and `IsClusterSecretStoreReady` are sufficient for the `k-orc-clouds-yaml` flow. If not, extend in the same file with shape-compatible signatures.
- `deploy/flux-system/namespaces.yaml` — add the `c5c3-system` Namespace for the c5c3-operator pod (mirrors `keystone-system` rationale at `namespaces.yaml:9-15`); add the `orc-system` Namespace where K-ORC and its `k-orc-clouds-yaml` Secret live.
- `deploy/flux-system/sources/k-orc.yaml` (new) and `deploy/flux-system/releases/k-orc.yaml` (new) — K-ORC `HelmRepository` and `HelmRelease` with `globalCloudConfig.secretName: k-orc-clouds-yaml`, plus `deploy/flux-system/releases/c5c3-operator.yaml` (mirrors `keystone-operator.yaml` shape with `dependsOn: keystone-operator + external-secrets + mariadb-operator + memcached-operator + k-orc`). Update `deploy/flux-system/kustomization.yaml:33-50` `resources:` to include all three new files.
- `deploy/eso/externalsecrets/k-orc-clouds-yaml.yaml` (new) — the `ExternalSecret` materialising `k-orc-clouds-yaml` into `orc-system` from the operator-pushed OpenBao path (e.g. `openstack/keystone/admin/app-credential`); update `deploy/eso/kustomization.yaml:13-17` `resources:` to include it.
- `deploy/openbao/policies/push-app-credentials.hcl:9-11` — verify the existing `kv-v2/data/openstack/*/app-credential` glob covers the chosen path. If the operator writes to `kv-v2/data/openstack/keystone/admin/app-credential`, this is already authorised by the existing glob; document the chosen path in an inline comment. If the chosen path falls outside `openstack/*/app-credential`, add a narrowly-scoped path to this file rather than widening the existing glob.
- `.github/workflows/ci.yaml:132` — add `c5c3` to `ALL_OPERATORS` so `e2e-operator`/`build-e2e-images` matrix expands to cover c5c3. Add a `c5c3:` paths-filter block alongside `keystone:` at `ci.yaml:76-78` and a matching `FILTER_c5c3` env var at `:133-142`.
- `.github/workflows/build-images.yaml:46-56` — add `operators/c5c3/Dockerfile` to the `lint-dockerfiles.matrix.dockerfile` list and update the `paths:` triggers (`build-images.yaml:13-34`) so changes to `operators/c5c3/Dockerfile` rebuild the image.

**Non-Goals:**

- **Per-pod / per-replica workload service users** (`ServiceUserSpec`, `User`/`Role` CRs per slot, env injection `OS_KEYSTONE_AUTHTOKEN__*`, finalizer + sweeper GC). This slice intentionally manages only K-ORC's own admin credential. `ServiceUsersReady` is omitted from `subConditionTypes`; the issue body labels this roadmap P2 and explicitly carves it out.
- **Other service operators** (Nova, Neutron, Glance, Cinder, Placement). `operators/` contains `c5c3` and `keystone` only today; adding any second OpenStack service operator is a separate issue. The c5c3 reconciler is designed to extend but only Keystone is wired here.
- **`SecretAggregate` reconciler** (from #366). Types are scaffolded so the API surface stabilises, but no controller is registered. The reconciler lands in a follow-up.
- **Full update-phase / rollback state machine** beyond the phases needed for the slice. `UpdatePhase` is plumbed in `ControlPlaneStatus` as an enum surface for future use, but `UpdatingServices`/`Verifying`/`RollingBack` transitions are not implemented — those land with the wider multi-service set.
- **`maxAge` rolling recreation and Ephemeral mode.** Roadmap P3/P4. The `ControlPlane` CRD does not introduce these fields.
- **Scheduled re-mint of the admin Application Credential.** The `CredentialRotation` bootstrap + password-driven re-mint paths are mandatory; the scheduled loop with `intervalDays`/`preRotationDays`/`gracePeriodDays` may land as an immediate fast-follow if the PR bloats — but if so, this decision must be called out explicitly in the PR description, not silently dropped.
- **Changing existing keystone-operator resources.** The c5c3 reconciler creates and owns Keystone CRs; it does not modify `operators/keystone/api/v1alpha1/` types, `operators/keystone/internal/controller/` reconciler shape, or the existing `tests/e2e/keystone/` suites.
- **Modifying the existing OpenBao policy authorisation surface.** If the chosen K-ORC admin App Cred path is under `kv-v2/data/openstack/*/app-credential`, the existing `deploy/openbao/policies/push-app-credentials.hcl:9-11` glob already covers it. If the chosen path is outside this glob, a narrowly-scoped path is added rather than widening the existing one.
- **A `c5c3-operator` ServiceMonitor.** The keystone-operator chart's `monitoring.serviceMonitor.enabled: false` opt-in pattern (`operators/keystone/helm/keystone-operator/values.yaml:37-40`) is the convention; a c5c3-operator ServiceMonitor lands when CC-0100's prometheus opt-in proves it for keystone.

make build/test/generate/manifests/sync-crds iterate c5c3 (already guarded by if [ -d operators/$op/api ])
cleanup-e2e-tags matrix includes c5c3-operator

stories.title: Engineer reads accurate reference documentation for the new CRD and reconciler
stories.want: docs/reference/c5c3/controlplane-crd.md and controlplane-reconciler.md mirroring the keystone reference docs, plus a K-ORC + c5c3-operator section in infrastructure-manifests.md
stories.so_that: an implementer or operator can understand the ControlPlane API surface, the reconciliation flow, conditions and the credential chain without reading the source
criteria: controlplane-crd.md documents API group/version, spec field tables (Field|Type|Required|Default|Description), CEL/webhook validation, and status conditions
controlplane-reconciler.md documents controller registration, Owns/Watches, the phased sub-reconcilers, condition aggregation and the credential chain
infrastructure-manifests.md gains a K-ORC + c5c3-operator section matching the existing structure
No files under the architecture/ submodule are edited; only the submodule pointer is bumped (or the upstream PR is linked)

requirements.description: The system SHALL define ControlPlane/ControlPlaneList, CredentialRotation/CredentialRotationList, and types-only SecretAggregate/SecretAggregateList Go types under group c5c3.io version v1alpha1 (groupversion_info.go) that compile, with controller-gen emitting zz_generated.deepcopy.go and CRD YAMLs cleanly.
requirements.rationale: No ControlPlane/KORCSpec/AdminCredentialSpec/CredentialRotation/SecretAggregate type exists anywhere; the entire API surface is created fresh and must round-trip through make generate/manifests/sync-crds.

scenarios.name: Types compile and generate
scenarios.when: operators/c5c3/api/v1alpha1 exists and make generate is run
scenarios.then: zz_generated.deepcopy.go is re-emitted with no diff on a second run and the module compiles
and_then: make manifests emits controlplanes.c5c3.io.yaml, credentialrotations.c5c3.io.yaml and secretaggregates.c5c3.io.yaml into operators/c5c3/config/crd/bases/

scenarios.name: CRDs sync into the chart
scenarios.then: the generated CRD YAMLs are copied into operators/c5c3/helm/c5c3-operator/crds/ and make verify-crd-sync is green
and_then: the Makefile if [ -d operators/$op/api ] guard now activates c5c3 without Makefile edits

scenarios.name: GroupVersion is correct
scenarios.then: GroupVersion is schema.GroupVersion{Group: "c5c3.io", Version: "v1alpha1"} with an AddToScheme function mirroring operators/keystone/api/v1alpha1/groupversion_info.go
and_then: root markers +kubebuilder:object:root=true and +kubebuilder:subresource:status are present on ControlPlane and CredentialRotation

requirements.description: ControlPlaneSpec SHALL contain openStackRelease (validated by +kubebuilder:validation:Pattern=`^\d{4}\.\d$`), region (defaulted to RegionOne), infrastructure.{database,cache} (reusing commonv1.DatabaseSpec/CacheSpec), services.keystone, global, and korc (KORCSpec).
requirements.rationale: The reconciler projects these fields into MariaDB/Memcached/Keystone; reusing commonv1 types keeps projection a near-trivial struct copy (issue point 10).

scenarios.name: Valid spec accepted
scenarios.when: a ControlPlane with openStackRelease="2024.1" and infrastructure.database.clusterRef set is applied
scenarios.then: the object is admitted
and_then: region defaults to RegionOne if omitted

scenarios.name: Bad release rejected
scenarios.then: the CRD pattern validation rejects it with a clear message
and_then: no object is persisted

scenarios.name: commonv1 reuse
scenarios.then: they embed commonv1.DatabaseSpec (clusterRef|host XOR) and commonv1.CacheSpec (clusterRef|servers XOR) verbatim
and_then: services.keystone is projectable into keystonev1alpha1.KeystoneSpec

requirements.description: AdminCredentialSpec SHALL carry cloudCredentialsRef (cloudName, secretName defaulted to k-orc-clouds-yaml), passwordSecretRef, applicationCredential (restricted defaulted true, optional accessRules, rotation.mode defaulted PasswordDriven), and bootstrapResources; KORCSpec SHALL embed adminCredential of type AdminCredentialSpec.
requirements.rationale: The K-ORC admin App Cred shape is baked in from the start, replacing the pre-K-ORC #364-#368 drafts.

scenarios.name: Defaults applied
scenarios.when: a ControlPlane with korc.adminCredential.passwordSecretRef set but restricted/rotation.mode omitted is applied
scenarios.then: the defaulting webhook sets applicationCredential.restricted=true and rotation.mode=PasswordDriven
and_then: cloudCredentialsRef.secretName defaults to k-orc-clouds-yaml

scenarios.name: Missing passwordSecretRef rejected
scenarios.then: the validating webhook rejects the object
and_then: the error names the missing field

scenarios.name: Restricted carried to CR
scenarios.when: the reconciler mints the K-ORC ApplicationCredential
scenarios.then: the CR carries the literal restricted: true field
and_then: accessRules are passed through when present

requirements.description: ControlPlaneStatus SHALL carry Conditions ([]metav1.Condition), an updatePhase enum surface, a services map, K-ORC admin App Cred fields (id/restricted/lastRotation), and catalog readiness; each sub-reconciler condition SHALL stamp ObservedGeneration.
requirements.rationale: Mirrors KeystoneStatus and satisfies the project drift guard; updatePhase is plumbed as a future-use enum (UpdatingServices/Verifying/RollingBack not implemented).

scenarios.name: Aggregate Ready
scenarios.then: Status.Conditions has Ready=True with reason AllReady
and_then: printer column Ready shows True

scenarios.name: Partial readiness
scenarios.then: aggregate Ready is False with reason NotAllReady
and_then: the failing sub-condition message is surfaced

scenarios.name: ObservedGeneration stamped
scenarios.then: its ObservedGeneration equals the ControlPlane metadata.generation
and_then: stale conditions are detectable

requirements.description: The ControlPlane defaulting webhook SHALL set region=RegionOne, applicationCredential.restricted=true, and rotation.mode=PasswordDriven when omitted, registered behind the webhooks parameter via builder.WebhookManagedBy.
requirements.rationale: Mirrors keystone_webhook.go Default() patterns and the acceptance criteria for defaulting.

scenarios.name: Region defaulted
scenarios.then: Default() sets it to RegionOne
and_then: the kubebuilder marker also defaults it for non-webhook installs

scenarios.name: Idempotent defaulting
scenarios.when: Default() runs on an already-defaulted object
scenarios.then: no fields change
and_then: no spurious updates are generated

scenarios.name: Explicit values preserved
scenarios.then: Default() does not overwrite it
and_then: only zero-valued fields are filled

requirements.description: The ControlPlane validating webhook SHALL reject invalid openStackRelease, a database with both/neither clusterRef and host, a cache with both/neither clusterRef and servers, and a korc.adminCredential without passwordSecretRef, on create and update.
requirements.rationale: Defense-in-depth XOR validation mirrors keystone_webhook.go; CEL markers provide the first line, the webhook the second.

scenarios.name: Database XOR enforced
scenarios.when: infrastructure.database sets both clusterRef and host
scenarios.then: ValidateCreate returns an error
and_then: setting neither also errors

scenarios.name: Cache XOR enforced
scenarios.when: infrastructure.cache sets both clusterRef and servers
scenarios.then: ValidateCreate returns an error
and_then: the message identifies the conflicting fields

scenarios.name: Valid update accepted
scenarios.when: a valid spec is updated to another valid spec
scenarios.then: ValidateUpdate returns nil
and_then: openStackRelease pattern is re-checked on update

requirements.description: ControlPlaneReconciler SHALL reconcile in phase order (infrastructure -> keystone -> korc -> catalog), declaring subConditionTypes = {InfrastructureReady, KeystoneReady, KORCReady, AdminCredentialReady, CatalogReady} as a var referencing typed conditionType*Ready constants, with early-return on non-zero ctrl.Result and an aggregate Ready computed via conditions.AllTrue.
requirements.rationale: Mirrors keystone_controller.go sub-reconciler dispatch and aggregation; ServiceUsersReady is deferred (non-goal).

scenarios.name: Phase ordering
scenarios.then: the reconciler early-returns and does not run reconcileKeystone
and_then: a requeue is scheduled

scenarios.name: No inline literals
scenarios.then: every condition type is a typed constant, never an inline string
and_then: tests reference the same constants

scenarios.name: Aggregate flips True
scenarios.then: setReadyCondition sets Ready=True
and_then: a subsequent reconcile is a no-op

requirements.description: reconcileInfrastructure SHALL project spec.infrastructure.{database,cache} into a MariaDB (k8s.mariadb.com/v1alpha1) CR and a Memcached (memcached.c5c3.io/v1beta1) CR in the openstack namespace, set owner references, set InfrastructureReady from their readiness, and own them via SetupWithManager.Owns.
requirements.rationale: Names/namespace/shape are proven by deploy/flux-system/infrastructure/{mariadb,memcached}.yaml (issue point 5).

scenarios.name: Infra CRs created
scenarios.when: a ControlPlane with managed (clusterRef) database/cache is reconciled
scenarios.then: MariaDB and Memcached CRs are created with controller owner references
and_then: serverCertIssuerRef/clientCertIssuerRef point at openstack-db-ca-issuer matching the static baseline

scenarios.name: Readiness gate
scenarios.when: the MariaDB CR is not yet Ready
scenarios.then: InfrastructureReady is False and the reconciler requeues
and_then: KeystoneReady is not evaluated

scenarios.name: Brownfield mode
scenarios.then: no MariaDB CR is created and InfrastructureReady reflects the external host being usable
and_then: the same applies to cache.servers

requirements.description: reconcileKeystone SHALL project spec.services.keystone into a keystonev1alpha1.Keystone CR: image tag derived from openStackRelease, Database/Cache clusterRefs wired to the infra CRs, PolicyOverrides from projectPolicyOverrides(global, perService), and Fernet/CredentialKeys RotationSchedule from intervalToCron(rotationInterval); set owner ref and KeystoneReady from CR readiness.
requirements.rationale: KeystoneSpec already accepts all projected fields (issue point 4); image tag comes from the release matrix.

scenarios.name: Projection correct
scenarios.when: a ControlPlane with openStackRelease=2024.1 is reconciled
scenarios.then: the Keystone CR Image.Tag is derived from the release and Database/Cache clusterRefs reference the infra CRs
and_then: merged policy is applied to PolicyOverrides

scenarios.name: Rotation converted
scenarios.then: the Keystone CR Fernet.RotationSchedule is "0 0 * * 0"
and_then: CredentialKeys.RotationSchedule is converted the same way

scenarios.name: Keystone readiness gate
scenarios.when: the Keystone CR is not yet Ready
scenarios.then: KeystoneReady is False and reconcileKORC is not run
and_then: a requeue is scheduled

requirements.description: reconcileKORC SHALL mint the admin Application Credential via a K-ORC ApplicationCredential CR (managed mode) carrying restricted: true and rotation.mode=PasswordDriven, owned by the ControlPlane, and set KORCReady from the CR readiness.
requirements.rationale: K-ORC is the single OpenStack client; the admin App Cred is minted from the admin password root of trust.

scenarios.name: App Cred minted restricted
scenarios.when: Keystone is Ready and the App Cred is absent
scenarios.then: a K-ORC ApplicationCredential CR is created with restricted: true
and_then: the App Cred ID/restricted are recorded in Status

scenarios.name: Idempotent
scenarios.when: the App Cred already exists and the password is unchanged
scenarios.then: no new CR is created
and_then: KORCReady stays True

scenarios.name: Missing K-ORC CRD
scenarios.when: the ApplicationCredential CRD is not installed
scenarios.then: the reconciler surfaces a clear error and requeues rather than panicking
and_then: KORCReady is False with a descriptive reason

requirements.description: reconcileKORC SHALL commit the minted credential to an operator-owned Secret, push it to OpenBao at openstack/keystone/admin/app-credential via secrets.EnsurePushSecret (ClusterSecretStore openbao-cluster-store), and gate AdminCredentialReady on secrets.WaitForExternalSecret reporting the k-orc-clouds-yaml ExternalSecret Ready in orc-system.
requirements.rationale: Mirrors the reconcile_passwordrotation.go Model B PushSecret chain and reuses the readiness helpers (issue point 7).

scenarios.name: Push chain link-by-link
scenarios.when: the App Cred Secret is valid
scenarios.then: a PushSecret with SecretStoreRefs[]{Kind: ClusterSecretStore, Name: openbao-cluster-store} and RemoteKey openstack/keystone/admin/app-credential is ensured
and_then: the ExternalSecret materialises k-orc-clouds-yaml into orc-system

scenarios.name: Readiness gate
scenarios.then: AdminCredentialReady is False and the reconciler requeues
and_then: IsClusterSecretStoreReady is checked first

scenarios.name: Clobber-safe
scenarios.when: the operator-owned Secret has no valid payload yet
scenarios.then: the PushSecret is not created (no empty overwrite of the OpenBao path)
and_then: the gate logs why it is waiting

requirements.description: The admin App Cred SHALL be re-minted when the admin password Secret's password byte hashes differently from the digest stored on the ApplicationCredential CR's pod-template-style annotation (mirroring adminPasswordHashAnnotation forge.c5c3.io/admin-password-hash), driven by a Watches on the admin password Secret with a field-indexer + MapFunc.
requirements.rationale: Re-mint on password change is mandatory for this slice; the hash annotation forces the re-mint exactly like reconcile_bootstrap.go forces Job re-run.

scenarios.name: Re-mint on change
scenarios.when: the admin password Secret password changes (new SHA-256)
scenarios.then: the watch enqueues the ControlPlane and the App Cred is re-minted
and_then: the new hash is stamped on the ApplicationCredential CR annotation

scenarios.name: No-op when unchanged
scenarios.then: no re-mint occurs
and_then: the reconcile is idempotent

scenarios.name: Indexed watch
scenarios.then: the field-indexer/MapFunc does not enqueue the ControlPlane
and_then: no O(n) Secret list happens on every event

requirements.description: The operator SHALL preserve the invariant that the admin password Secret and the admin App Cred Secret are never mounted (env or volume) in any Deployment/StatefulSet/DaemonSet owned transitively by the ControlPlane, except the c5c3-operator and K-ORC controller pods.
requirements.rationale: Bounded blast radius (OWASP A01/A07); mirrors keystone reconcile_bootstrap.go where the admin password only enters the one-shot Job.

scenarios.name: Workloads clean
scenarios.when: a test walks all owned workloads
scenarios.then: none references keystone-admin or the app-credential Secret in env/volumes
and_then: the test fails if such a reference is introduced

scenarios.name: Allowed pods excluded
scenarios.when: the c5c3-operator and K-ORC controller pods are evaluated
scenarios.then: they are excluded from the workload set under test
and_then: k-orc-clouds-yaml may be mounted in orc-system only

scenarios.name: Restricted credential
scenarios.then: it is restricted: true so any leak is scoped
and_then: the invariant test documents the allowed exceptions

requirements.description: reconcileKORC SHALL register the Keystone Service and Endpoint in the catalog via K-ORC Service/Endpoint CRs (owned by the ControlPlane) and set CatalogReady from their readiness.
requirements.rationale: A control plane with identity but no managed catalog has no path to project/role/domain bootstrap that survives recreation (motivation).

scenarios.name: Catalog registered
scenarios.when: the admin App Cred is Ready
scenarios.then: K-ORC Service and Endpoint CRs for Keystone are created and owned
and_then: CatalogReady flips True once they report Ready

scenarios.name: Gate on credential
scenarios.then: catalog registration is not attempted
and_then: CatalogReady stays False

scenarios.name: Idempotent
scenarios.then: no duplicates are created
and_then: the reconcile is a no-op

requirements.description: A CredentialRotationReconciler SHALL handle target=adminApplicationCredential: bootstrap (mint on absence) and re-mint (on admin password hash change). The scheduled intervalDays/preRotationDays/gracePeriodDays loop MAY land as a fast-follow if documented explicitly in the PR description.
requirements.rationale: Issue point 13 restricts CredentialRotation to adminApplicationCredential and marks the scheduled loop as an authorised fast-follow.

scenarios.name: Bootstrap mint
scenarios.when: a CredentialRotation(adminApplicationCredential) reconciles with no App Cred present
scenarios.then: it mints the App Cred
and_then: a typed condition records success

scenarios.name: Re-mint on change
scenarios.then: it re-mints
and_then: the new hash is recorded

scenarios.name: Scheduled deferred
scenarios.when: intervalDays is set but the scheduled loop is unimplemented
scenarios.then: the field is accepted but the PR description names the deferral
and_then: no silent drop occurs

requirements.description: helpers.intervalToCron(time.Duration) (string, error) SHALL convert supported intervals to cron (168h -> "0 0 * * 0", 24h -> "0 0 * * *") and return an error for unsupported intervals.
requirements.rationale: Translates ControlPlaneSpec rotationInterval into the cron the Keystone CR consumes (issue point 17); pure and table-testable.

scenarios.name: Weekly
scenarios.then: returns "0 0 * * 0", nil
and_then: the value is accepted by Keystone Fernet.RotationSchedule validation

scenarios.name: Daily
scenarios.then: returns "0 0 * * *", nil

scenarios.name: Unsupported
scenarios.then: returns a non-nil error
and_then: the empty string is returned alongside the error

requirements.description: helpers.projectPolicyOverrides(global *commonv1.PolicySpec, perService *commonv1.PolicySpec) *commonv1.PolicySpec SHALL merge global (base) with per-service overrides (overrides win on key conflict) into the commonv1.PolicySpec{Rules, ConfigMapRef} shape.
requirements.rationale: Merges spec.global.policy with services.keystone.policyOverrides (issue point 17); pure and table-testable.

scenarios.name: Override wins
scenarios.when: global and perService both set rule key K
scenarios.then: the result has perService's value for K
and_then: non-conflicting global rules are retained

scenarios.name: Nil inputs
scenarios.then: the result is nil (or empty)
and_then: no panic occurs

scenarios.name: One side only
scenarios.then: the result equals global
and_then: ConfigMapRef precedence is documented and tested

requirements.description: SecretAggregate SHALL be scaffolded as Go types + deepcopy + CRD YAML only; no controller is registered in main.go and the chart ClusterRole SHALL grant only get/list/watch (no write verbs) on secretaggregates.c5c3.io.
requirements.rationale: Issue point 18 / non-goal: the reconciler is deferred to CC-0023; only the API surface stabilises.

scenarios.name: Type exists, no controller
scenarios.then: no SecretAggregate controller is registered
and_then: the CRD YAML exists under config/crd/bases and the chart crds/

scenarios.name: Read-only RBAC
scenarios.then: secretaggregates has only get/list/watch
and_then: no +kubebuilder:rbac write markers exist for secretaggregates

scenarios.name: Deepcopy generated
scenarios.then: SecretAggregate deepcopy methods are emitted
and_then: the type is registered in the scheme

requirements.description: operators/c5c3/main.go SHALL register all required schemes (c5c3v1alpha1, keystonev1alpha1, mariadbv1alpha1, memcachedv1beta1, esov1+esov1alpha1, orcv1alpha1), wire ControlPlaneReconciler and CredentialRotationReconciler, register the defaulting+validation webhooks behind the webhooks parameter, and remove the // +kubebuilder:scaffold:builder placeholder body, calling bootstrap.Run with LeaderElectionID c5c3.openstack.c5c3.io.
requirements.rationale: Mirrors operators/keystone/main.go; the SetupFunc receives (mgr, webhooks bool).

scenarios.name: Schemes registered
scenarios.then: all CRD kinds it interacts with resolve without 'no kind is registered' errors
and_then: the gateway scheme is optional

scenarios.name: Webhooks gated
scenarios.then: webhooks are not registered (SetupFunc honours the webhooks bool)
and_then: controllers still register

scenarios.name: Probes unchanged
scenarios.then: /healthz and /readyz respond on the bootstrap health-probe-bind-address
and_then: metrics flags are unchanged from bootstrap defaults

requirements.description: operators/c5c3/Dockerfile SHALL be a multi-stage build (golang:1.26 builder copying the go.work workspace -> gcr.io/distroless/static:nonroot runtime) built from the workspace root context, added to the build-images.yaml lint-dockerfiles matrix and paths triggers, and passing hadolint --config .hadolint.yaml.
requirements.rationale: Mirrors operators/keystone/Dockerfile; the workspace build needs go.work + all module go.mod/go.sum copied.

scenarios.name: Builds
scenarios.when: docker build -f operators/c5c3/Dockerfile is run from the workspace root
scenarios.then: the manager binary is produced and copied into the distroless runtime
and_then: it copies go.work, internal/common, operators/keystone and operators/c5c3 like the keystone Dockerfile

scenarios.name: Lints clean
scenarios.then: no findings are reported
and_then: the file is in the lint-dockerfiles matrix

scenarios.name: Triggers rebuild
scenarios.then: build-images.yaml paths triggers rebuild the image

requirements.description: operators/c5c3/helm/c5c3-operator/ SHALL render cleanly (helm template), pass helm lint --strict and helm-unittest, derive its ClusterRole from the reconciler +kubebuilder:rbac markers, wire webhook TLS via cert-manager.io/inject-ca-from, expose monitoring.serviceMonitor as an opt-in (default false), and publish to oci://ghcr.io/c5c3/charts.
requirements.rationale: Cloned and stripped from the keystone-operator chart blueprint (issue point 12); values.schema.json is draft-07 with additionalProperties:false.

scenarios.name: Renders and lints
scenarios.when: helm template + helm lint --strict run against values.yaml
scenarios.then: both succeed
and_then: helm-unittest tests under templates/tests/ pass

scenarios.name: RBAC matches markers
scenarios.when: the rendered ClusterRole is compared to the +kubebuilder:rbac markers
scenarios.then: verbs/resources match (controlplanes, credentialrotations, mariadbs, memcacheds, keystones, k-orc applicationcredentials/services/endpoints, secrets, pushsecrets, externalsecrets, clustersecretstores)
and_then: secretaggregates is get/list/watch only

scenarios.name: No ServiceMonitor by default
scenarios.then: monitoring.serviceMonitor.enabled is false
and_then: a ServiceMonitor renders only when enabled

requirements.description: .github/workflows/ci.yaml SHALL add c5c3 to ALL_OPERATORS (line 132), add a c5c3: paths-filter block (operators/c5c3/** and images/k-orc/** if any) mirroring the keystone block, add a FILTER_c5c3 env var, and update cleanup-e2e-tags to include c5c3-operator; the test/test-integration matrices already include c5c3.
requirements.rationale: Issue point 2: c5c3 is in the unit/integration matrices but ALL_OPERATORS is still keystone-only, so e2e/build-e2e-images do not cover it.

scenarios.name: E2E matrix expands
scenarios.when: ALL_OPERATORS includes c5c3 and a c5c3 path changes
scenarios.then: ci-resolve-changes.sh emits c5c3 in the e2e-operators matrix
and_then: the e2e-operator job runs the c5c3 leg

scenarios.name: Paths filter triggers
scenarios.then: FILTER_c5c3 is true and downstream jobs fire
and_then: unrelated changes do not trigger c5c3 e2e

scenarios.name: Tag cleanup
scenarios.then: cleanup-e2e-tags removes c5c3-operator tags
and_then: no GHCR quota accumulation

requirements.description: The GitOps stack SHALL add the c5c3-system and orc-system namespaces, deploy/flux-system/sources/k-orc.yaml + releases/k-orc.yaml (globalCloudConfig.secretName: k-orc-clouds-yaml), deploy/flux-system/releases/c5c3-operator.yaml (dependsOn keystone-operator+external-secrets+mariadb-operator+memcached-operator+k-orc), all listed in deploy/flux-system/kustomization.yaml, plus deploy/eso/externalsecrets/k-orc-clouds-yaml.yaml listed in deploy/eso/kustomization.yaml.
requirements.rationale: Issue point 11 + Affected Areas; mirrors the keystone-operator HelmRelease shape and the ESO ExternalSecret template.

scenarios.name: Namespaces declared
scenarios.then: c5c3-system and orc-system exist
and_then: existing namespaces are unchanged

scenarios.name: Dependency ordering
scenarios.then: c5c3-operator installs only after its dependsOn releases are Ready
and_then: k-orc installs with globalCloudConfig.secretName: k-orc-clouds-yaml

scenarios.name: ESO materialises
scenarios.when: the operator has pushed the app-credential to OpenBao
scenarios.then: the k-orc-clouds-yaml ExternalSecret syncs it into orc-system
and_then: the ExternalSecret is in deploy/eso/kustomization.yaml

requirements.description: deploy/openbao/policies/push-app-credentials.hcl SHALL authorise the chosen K-ORC admin App Cred write path. Because the existing glob kv-v2/data/openstack/*/app-credential uses a mid-path wildcard that does NOT match the deeper path openstack/keystone/admin/app-credential, a narrowly-scoped path SHALL be added (not the existing glob widened); the chosen path SHALL be documented in an inline comment.
requirements.rationale: Research confirmed the mid-path * does not match the two-segment keystone/admin path; the non-goal forbids widening the existing glob.

scenarios.name: Write path authorised
scenarios.then: the OpenBao policy grants create/update/read on exactly that path
and_then: an inline comment records the chosen path and why a new path was added

scenarios.name: Read path covered
scenarios.when: the eso-management role reads the same path
scenarios.then: the existing trailing-glob kv-v2/data/openstack/keystone/* covers the read
and_then: no change to eso-management.hcl is required

scenarios.name: Glob not widened
scenarios.then: the original openstack/*/app-credential glob is unchanged
and_then: only a new narrowly-scoped path is added

requirements.description: Reference docs docs/reference/c5c3/{controlplane-crd,controlplane-reconciler}.md SHALL be created mirroring the keystone reference docs, and docs/reference/infrastructure/infrastructure-manifests.md SHALL gain a K-ORC + c5c3-operator section; the architecture/ submodule SHALL NOT be edited (only its pointer bumped or the upstream PR linked).
requirements.rationale: Diataxis reference quadrant; architecture/ is a read-only submodule (critical-rule 12).

scenarios.name: CRD doc structure
scenarios.then: it has spec field tables (Field|Type|Required|Default|Description), validation rules and status conditions like keystone-crd.md
and_then: front matter + single H1 + intro present

scenarios.name: Reconciler doc
scenarios.then: it documents Owns/Watches, phased sub-reconcilers, condition aggregation and the credential chain
and_then: cross-references resolve

scenarios.name: Submodule untouched
scenarios.then: no files under architecture/ are modified
and_then: the submodule pointer bump or upstream PR link is noted in the PR description

requirements.description: Each sub-reconciler SHALL be wrapped in instrumentSubReconciler with a c5c3 metrics package (c5c3_operator_* prefix mirroring operators/keystone/internal/metrics), and every condition type SHALL appear in the subReconcilerConditionTypes map; a drift-guard test SHALL assert full coverage and no CR labels on sub-reconciler metrics.
requirements.rationale: Mirrors operators/keystone/internal/controller/instrumentation.go and the cardinality drift guards.

scenarios.name: Metrics emitted
scenarios.then: c5c3_operator_reconcile_duration_seconds is observed with a sub_reconciler label
and_then: errors increment c5c3_operator_reconcile_errors_total with {sub_reconciler, condition_type}

scenarios.name: Drift guard
scenarios.when: a sub-reconciler name is missing from subReconcilerConditionTypes
scenarios.then: the coverage test fails
and_then: the condition_type label never silently becomes UNKNOWN in production

scenarios.name: No CR labels
scenarios.then: no controlplane/namespace labels exist on sub-reconciler duration/error metrics
and_then: cardinality stays bounded

requirements.description: An envtest integration suite (operators/c5c3/internal/controller/integration_test.go, //go:build integration) SHALL drive create -> infra simulated Ready -> Keystone projection + simulated Ready -> ApplicationCredential/Service/Endpoint created + simulated Ready -> ControlPlane.Status.Ready=True with AdminCredentialReady/CatalogReady, reusing the shared envtest setup and simulators, runnable via make test-integration OPERATOR=c5c3.
requirements.rationale: The project has an integration tier; mirrors operators/keystone/internal/controller/integration_test.go and the simulators package.

scenarios.name: Full chain
scenarios.when: a ControlPlane is applied and simulators flip child CRs Ready in order
scenarios.then: ControlPlane.Status.Ready becomes True
and_then: AdminCredentialReady and CatalogReady are True

scenarios.name: Projection asserted
scenarios.then: image tag, clusterRefs and merged policy match the projection
and_then: the rotation schedule is the converted cron

scenarios.name: Skips when unavailable
scenarios.then: the suite calls SkipIfEnvTestUnavailable(t) and skips
and_then: no false failure

requirements.description: A Chainsaw E2E suite at tests/e2e/c5c3/full-controlplane-keystone/chainsaw-test.yaml SHALL apply a ControlPlane on a real kind cluster and assert the chain link-by-link: infra CRs -> Keystone CR (correct tag/clusterRefs/policy) -> K-ORC ApplicationCredential (restricted:true) -> k-orc-clouds-yaml in orc-system -> Service/Endpoint registered -> ControlPlane Ready=True, plus an HTTP 200 from /v3 and an admin token issue, following the one-feature-one-directory convention.
requirements.rationale: The project has an E2E tier (44 keystone suites); the c5c3 suite runs under the e2e-operator matrix once c5c3 is in ALL_OPERATORS.

scenarios.name: End-to-end Ready
scenarios.when: the ControlPlane is applied on kind
scenarios.then: every step asserts Ready/synced before the next is checked and ControlPlane Ready=True
and_then: openstack catalog list shows the Keystone Service/Endpoint

scenarios.name: API reachable
scenarios.then: HTTP 200 is returned
and_then: openstack token issue with the admin password succeeds

scenarios.name: Secret chain visible
scenarios.when: the KV path and ExternalSecret are checked
scenarios.then: the eso-management role can read the path and the k-orc-clouds-yaml ExternalSecret is Ready=True
and_then: AdminCredentialReady=True is asserted

implementation_notes:
ARCHITECTURE: The c5c3-operator is an orchestration operator. One ControlPlane CR (group c5c3.io/v1alpha1) is reconciled by ControlPlaneReconciler through phased sub-reconcilers that PROJECT spec into, and OWN, child CRs managed by other operators. It does not implement Keystone/MariaDB/Memcached logic; it composes them. Follow the KeystoneReconciler reference shape exactly (operators/keystone/internal/controller/keystone_controller.go).

COMPONENT DIAGRAM (3+ components):

  ControlPlane (c5c3.io/v1alpha1)
      | reconcileInfrastructure  -> Owns MariaDB (k8s.mariadb.com/v1alpha1) + Memcached (memcached.c5c3.io/v1beta1)  [ns: openstack]
      | reconcileKeystone        -> Owns Keystone (keystone.openstack.c5c3.io/v1alpha1)  (projection via intervalToCron + projectPolicyOverrides)
      | reconcileKORC            -> Owns K-ORC ApplicationCredential / Service / Endpoint (openstack.k-orc.cloud/v1alpha1)
      |                            -> operator-owned Secret -> EnsurePushSecret -> OpenBao(openstack/keystone/admin/app-credential) -> ExternalSecret -> k-orc-clouds-yaml [ns: orc-system]
      v
  Status.Conditions: InfrastructureReady, KeystoneReady, KORCReady, AdminCredentialReady, CatalogReady -> aggregate Ready (conditions.AllTrue)

DATA FLOW (admin credential backbone, P0+P1):
  kv-v2/bootstrap/keystone-admin (root of trust, seeded by write-bootstrap-secrets.sh)
    -> ExternalSecret keystone-admin -> keystone-admin Secret (read by c5c3-operator only)
    -> c5c3-operator mints K-ORC ApplicationCredential (restricted:true) from the admin password
    -> commits App Cred to operator-owned Secret -> PushSecret(openbao-cluster-store) -> OpenBao openstack/keystone/admin/app-credential
    -> ExternalSecret k-orc-clouds-yaml -> k-orc-clouds-yaml Secret in orc-system (read by K-ORC controller only)
  Re-mint trigger: SHA-256(admin password) compared to forge.c5c3.io/admin-password-hash annotation on the ApplicationCredential CR.

KEY DECISIONS / PITFALLS:
1. OpenBao policy glob: the existing push-app-credentials.hcl uses kv-v2/data/openstack/*/app-credential, where the MID-PATH * does NOT match the two-segment path openstack/keystone/admin/app-credential. ADD a narrowly-scoped path (do not widen the glob). The eso-management.hcl read uses a TRAILING glob kv-v2/data/openstack/keystone/* which DOES cover the read. (REQ-024)
2. Cross-module imports: c5c3 must import keystonev1alpha1 from operators/keystone via a go.mod require + replace (=> ../keystone), like the existing internal/common replace. mariadb (v0.38.1) and external-secrets/apis pins must MATCH operators/keystone/go.mod and internal/common/go.mod to keep the workspace in lockstep (see check-go-workspace-deps).
3. memcached.c5c3.io/v1beta1 and K-ORC (openstack.k-orc.cloud) Go types are imported NOWHERE today. Resolve their Go module paths; if no importable module exists, fall back to unstructured.Unstructured with explicit GVK for Owns()/create (controller-runtime supports this), and leave a # DECISION comment. This is the single biggest integration risk.
4. The admin-credential invariant is a hard requirement: read keystone-admin and the app-credential Secret only in the c5c3-operator and K-ORC controller pods; never mount them in Keystone or service-user workloads. Enforce with the workload-walk test (REQ-013), mirroring reconcile_bootstrap.go where the admin password only enters the one-shot Job.
5. Condition<->metric drift: keystone has TestSubReconcilerConditionTypesCoversAllNames. Replicate it for c5c3 so a new phase added without a map entry fails CI rather than silently emitting condition_type=UNKNOWN.
6. SecretAggregate is types-only (non-goal): no controller, no write RBAC; chart ClusterRole get/list/watch only.
7. Scheduled CredentialRotation loop (intervalDays/preRotationDays/gracePeriodDays) is an authorised fast-follow; if deferred, the PR description MUST say so.
8. Use commonv1 types verbatim (DatabaseSpec/CacheSpec/PolicySpec/ImageSpec/SecretRefSpec) so projection is a struct copy, not a translation layer.

DEFERRED ITEMS (explicitly out of scope, with rationale and revisit timing):
- Per-pod/per-replica workload service users (ServiceUserSpec, User/Role CRs, env injection, finalizer sweeper) and the ServiceUsersReady condition. Rationale: roadmap P2; this slice manages only K-ORC's admin credential. Revisit: when the second service operator (Nova) lands.
- Other service operators (Nova/Neutron/Glance/Cinder/Placement). Rationale: operators/ has only c5c3+keystone; the reconciler is designed to extend. Revisit: per-service follow-up issues.
- SecretAggregate reconciler. Rationale: API surface only (CC-0023). Revisit: CC-0023.
- Full UpdatePhase state machine (UpdatingServices/Verifying/RollingBack). Rationale: the enum is plumbed for future use; transitions need the multi-service set. Revisit: with multi-service lifecycle.
- maxAge rolling recreation / Ephemeral mode. Rationale: roadmap P3/P4; fields not introduced.
- Scheduled re-mint loop for the admin App Cred. Rationale: bootstrap + password-driven re-mint are mandatory; scheduled loop is an immediate fast-follow if the PR bloats (must be called out, not dropped).
- c5c3-operator ServiceMonitor. Rationale: follows the keystone monitoring.serviceMonitor opt-in (default false) convention; lands when CC-0100 proves it for keystone.

OBSERVABILITY/SECURITY/SCALABILITY: structured logging via the shared bootstrap zap logger; every sub-reconciler stamps ObservedGeneration on its condition and emits Recorder events; c5c3_operator_* metrics with bounded cardinality (no CR labels on sub-reconciler duration/error). Single root-of-trust with bounded blast radius via restricted App Cred and absence from workload pods (OWASP A01/A07, Twelve-Factor III). Stateless operator; leader election via bootstrap (LeaderElectionID c5c3.openstack.c5c3.io); idempotent reconciliation with owner-ref-driven GC.
